compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PARTIALLY DONE (2 of 3). Part 1 (import-cycle CI strangler gate, scripts/check_import_cycles.py + grimp baseline) merged via change 208. Part 2 (reducer->_engine_support inversion fixed via the rebar._ids leaf) merged via change 205. Part 3 (9.3: route leaf reads through rebar._reads instead of the facade) is NOT done — CHECKPOINTED. Leaving this ticket OPEN until 9.3 lands.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
9.3 (part 3) execution plan — refined after grimp SCC analysis in worktree janitor/item-9.3-facade-reads:

FINDING: rebar._reads is ITSELF in the 36-module SCC. Its only ->rebar (facade) back-edge is the lazy 'from rebar import RebarError' (line 45); 'from rebar import config' is a submodule edge (->rebar.config, a leaf) and does NOT bind it to the facade. So the measurable cycle win comes from making _reads a true leaf.

PLAN:
1. New stdlib-only leaf src/rebar/_errors.py defining RebarError + ConcurrencyError (moved verbatim). __init__.py re-exports them ('from rebar._errors import ...') for full back-compat (rebar.RebarError / from rebar import RebarError unchanged).
2. _reads.py line 45 imports RebarError from the leaf -> _reads leaves the SCC. Verified via grimp simulation: largest SCC 36 -> 35, _reads removed; import-cycle baseline ratchets down.
3. Reroute each function-local 'import rebar' read site whose rebar.* calls are ALL exported by _reads (show_ticket/list_tickets/deps/ready/next_batch/search/recent_session_logs) to 'from rebar import _reads': spec_scan, operations, pass1, orchestrator, gate_ops fully drop 'import rebar'; completion/attest/pai_tools/steps reroute their pure-read function scopes and keep 'import rebar' only where the same scope also does writes (comment/tag/edit) or reads not in _reads (verify_signature/get_file_impact). Behavior identical.
4. scripts/check_import_cycles.py --update to ratchet the baseline; full suite + make lint/mypy green.

NOTE: the big 36->18 collapse would need routing WRITES through a leaf too — out of scope for a reads-only ticket; not attempted here.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review re-run: PASS (signed, 0 blocking). Advisories 15 -> 11 after remediation. Remediated the substantive cluster: (1) rewrote description so AC #1/#2 are marked DONE with their change numbers (was presenting shipped work as pending); (2) corrected the '~69-module' SCC premise to the live 36; (3) enumerated the exact target files for item 9.3 + the '_reads is itself in the SCC' finding + the make-_reads-a-leaf fix; (4) added a measurable AC (check_import_cycles reports smaller largest_scc_size 36->35 + baseline ratcheted). Set file_impact (13 paths). Residual advisories (rollback plan / MVP-first sequencing / import-linter-vs-grimp phrasing) are moot: parts 1&2 already shipped, so there is nothing left to sequence or roll back incrementally, and the tool choice (custom grimp baseline, not the import-linter pip pkg) is already the committed reality. Proceeding to claim.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implemented item 9.3 in worktree janitor/item-9.3-facade-reads. RESULT: largest import SCC 36->35 (rebar._reads left the cycle); cross-package cycle pairs 11->7 (removed _commands::_reads, _io::_reads, _reads::llm, _reads::rebar); modules-in-cycles 49->48. Baseline ratcheted. Changes: (1) new stdlib-only leaf src/rebar/_errors.py holds RebarError+ConcurrencyError; __init__ re-exports them (rebar.RebarError identity preserved); _reads sources RebarError from the leaf -> _reads is now a leaf. (2) Every function-local read call-site in llm/{spec_scan,operations,completion,pai_tools,plan_review/pass1,plan_review/orchestrator,plan_review/attest,workflow/steps,workflow/gate_ops} now uses 'from rebar import _reads'; mixed scopes keep 'import rebar' only for writes (comment/tag/edit) or verify_signature. (3) Tests: 34 read-mock sites retargeted from the facade re-export to the single seam rebar._reads (monkeypatch string form). Gates: make lint + make check (mypy) green; FULL suite 4324 passed / 48 skipped / 0 failed; import-cycle + reducer-inversion contract tests green. Pushing to Gerrit for review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit: change 232 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/232). Awaiting LLM-Review + Verified votes. Ticket stays in_progress until 232 submits to main.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: 232 CI (Verified -1) was NOT a code failure: the change was based on a stale origin/main (c66044058) predating the 'CI config-gate' commit that added the 'make config-check' target — the workflow (from current main) ran 'make config-check' against the old tree -> 'No rule to make target'. Fix: rebased onto current origin/main (clean; 9.3 touches no Makefile/CI), re-ran config-check + lint + mypy + import-cycle (all green), pushed patchset 2. LLM-Review was already +1. Awaiting re-vote.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Change 232 SUBMITTED — MERGED to main (2026-07-03). Both gates green: LLM-Review +1, Verified +1. Item 9.3 (facade-read reroute + _reads-as-leaf) is landed: largest import SCC 36->35, cross-package cycle pairs 11->7, baseline ratcheted. Closing ticket.

compiled_state.description:
# Reduce the import-cycle SCC (facade-read reroute — parts 1 & 2 landed; this ticket now = part 3 / item 9.3)

## Context
A verified AST+Tarjan analysis found a large strongly-connected import component spanning the
facade, `_commands`, `signing`, and most of `llm`, held together by function-local lazy imports.
Layering was enforced only by docstrings. Three remediations were approved. **Two have already
shipped to `main`; this ticket now tracks ONLY the remaining part 3 (item 9.3).** The live baseline
(`.import-cycle-baseline.json`) records the current surface: `largest_scc_size = 36`,
11 cross-package cycle pairs, 49 modules-in-cycles. (The original "~69-module" figure predated the
part-1/part-2 landings; 36 is the current truth.)

## What
1. **[DONE — change 208]** Import-cycle CI strangler contract: `scripts/check_import_cycles.py` +
   `.import-cycle-baseline.json` + CI wiring (`.github/workflows/test.yml`) + `tests/unit/
   test_import_cycles.py`. Freezes the surface; fails only NEW cross-package cycles.
2. **[DONE — change 205]** Reducer layering-inversion fix: the shared id/alias primitive lives in
   the leaf `src/rebar/_ids.py`; `_engine_support/resolver.py` re-exports it; `reducer/_processors.py`
   imports from `rebar._ids` (no longer UP into `_engine_support`). Guarded by
   `tests/unit/test_no_reducer_engine_support_cycle.py`.
3. **[THIS TICKET — item 9.3]** Route leaf **read** callers off the `rebar` facade onto `rebar._reads`,
   and make `rebar._reads` itself a leaf so the reroute measurably shrinks the SCC.

## Item 9.3 — concrete scope (grimp-verified in worktree janitor/item-9.3-facade-reads)
- `rebar._reads` is currently IN the 36-SCC. Its ONLY `→rebar` (facade) back-edge is the lazy
  `from rebar import RebarError` (line 45); `from rebar import config` is a submodule edge
  (`→rebar.config`, a leaf) and does not bind it to the facade.
- **Make `_reads` a leaf:** move `RebarError` + `ConcurrencyError` into a new stdlib-only leaf
  `src/rebar/_errors.py`; `rebar/__init__.py` re-exports them (full back-compat); `_reads` imports
  them from the leaf. grimp simulation: largest SCC **36 → 35** (`_reads` leaves the cycle); the
  import-cycle baseline ratchets down.
- **Reroute read call-sites:** for each function-local `import rebar` whose `rebar.*` calls are ALL
  exported by `_reads` (`show_ticket`/`list_tickets`/`deps`/`ready`/`next_batch`/`search`/
  `recent_session_logs`), switch that site to `from rebar import _reads`. Targeted files:
  `llm/spec_scan.py`, `llm/operations.py`, `llm/completion.py`, `llm/pai_tools.py`,
  `llm/plan_review/pass1.py`, `llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py`, `llm/plan_review/attest.py`,
  `llm/workflow/steps.py`, `llm/workflow/gate_ops.py`. `spec_scan`/`operations`/`pass1`/`orchestrator`/
  `gate_ops` drop `import rebar` entirely; the others keep it only in scopes that also do writes
  (`comment`/`tag`/`edit_ticket`) or reads not in `_reads` (`verify_signature`/`get_file_impact`).
- **Out of scope:** the larger 36→18 collapse needs routing WRITES through a leaf too — not a
  reads-only change; not attempted here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Import-cycle CI contract in place; passes current state, fails a new cross-package cycle (change 208).
- [x] `reducer` no longer imports `_engine_support`; shared resolution in the leaf `rebar._ids`; reducer/replay tests green (change 205).
- [ ] `rebar._reads` no longer imports the `rebar` facade (`RebarError` sourced from the new leaf `rebar._errors`); `rebar._reads` is OUT of the largest SCC.
- [ ] Read-only function-local `import rebar` sites in the enumerated `llm/*` modules use `from rebar import _reads`; no behavior change; suite green.
- [ ] Measurable: `python scripts/check_import_cycles.py` reports a SMALLER `largest_scc_size` than the pre-change baseline (36 → 35), and the baseline JSON is ratcheted down (`--update`) in the same commit.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` (mypy) + full suite green; commit carries a `rebar-ticket:` trailer.

file_impact.reason: new stdlib-only leaf: RebarError + ConcurrencyError moved here
source RebarError from the leaf so _reads becomes a leaf (out of SCC)
reroute read sites import rebar -> from rebar import _reads

compiled_state.title: Reduce the import-cycle SCC (import-linter contract + inversion fix + facade-read reroute)
